# AtariTest SPECIFICATION

## Overview

AtariTest is a 4KB NTSC Atari 2600 ROM, written in 6502 assembly and built with DASM, that demonstrates two-joystick control: each joystick drives a sprite with acceleration/friction movement and can fire a single-pixel missile in any of 8 directions. A title screen displays "TEST GAME" on power-on; pressing fire on either joystick starts gameplay. Verification is via Stella on macOS. See PRD.md for goals, requirements, and decisions.

## ProblemStatement

Demonstrate independent two-player joystick control on the Atari 2600: each joystick drives a sprite with momentum-based movement and can fire a missile in any of 8 directions, with a title screen and a runnable ROM produced via DASM. Acts as a controls and build-pipeline proof of concept rather than a full game.

## Goals

G1 -- Produce a 4KB NTSC ROM `build/ataritest.bin` built via DASM. G2 -- TEST GAME title screen on power-on; transition to gameplay on either fire button. G3 -- Taskfile-driven single-command build. G4 -- Manual smoke-test verification in Stella on macOS. Non-goals (v1): sound, score/HUD, lives, levels, AI opponents, game-over, save state, PAL, bankswitching, ROM > 4KB, scoring/respawn on hit.

## UserStories

US1 Move sprite via joystick with momentum. US2 Fire missile in joystick direction (8-way). US3 Edge-triggered fire (no auto-fire on hold). US4 See TEST GAME title on power-on. US5 Either fire button starts the game. US6 Build ROM with one command. US7 Run ROM in Stella on macOS.

## Requirements

Functional: FR-1 Title screen with PF "TEST GAME" on reset. FR-2 Either fire button transitions title -> play. FR-3 Title does not transition on direction-only input. FR-4 Each joystick maps to one sprite. FR-5 Held direction accelerates sprite to MAX_SPEED. FR-6 Released direction applies FRICTION toward 0. FR-7 Sprites clamp to NTSC playfield bounds. FR-8 Players differ by COLOR ONLY. FR-9 At most one active missile per player. FR-10 Edge-triggered fire only. FR-11 No fire when joystick is centered. FR-12 8-way missile direction. FR-13 Missile is a 1-pixel TIA missile. FR-14 Missile despawns at edge. FR-15 Hit on opponent triggers brief flash; play continues; missile consumed. FR-16 No score/lives/reset on hit. FR-17 `task build` invokes DASM. FR-18 Output is `build/ataritest.bin`. FR-19 4KB single-bank, no bankswitching. Non-functional: NFR-1 NTSC 60Hz stable (262 LN). NFR-2 Loads/runs in latest stable Stella on macOS. NFR-3 ROM <= 4KB. NFR-4 Reproducible byte-identical builds for fixed DASM version. NFR-5 macOS DASM install steps documented. NFR-6 Source organized by concern with subroutine comments.

## SuccessMetrics

SM-1 `task build` produces `build/ataritest.bin` exactly 4096 bytes. SM-2 ROM shows stable TEST GAME title in Stella at 60Hz. SM-3 Either fire transitions to play reliably. SM-4 Each joystick independently moves sprite with visible accel/friction; sprites stay on-screen. SM-5 8-way missile firing, 1 active per player, despawns at edge. SM-6 Hit on opponent triggers visible flash; play continues. SM-7 All 12 locked decisions reflected in implementation.

## EdgeCases

Diagonal input + fire: missile uses 8-way snapped direction. Joystick centered + fire pressed: no missile. Fire held: only first edge fires; subsequent presses require release. Sprite at boundary + accelerating outward: velocity clamped, position clamped. Missile collides with both opponent sprite and edge in same frame: collision wins (flash + despawn). Both players fire same frame: both missiles spawn independently. Missile spawned at sprite center; edge despawn after position update. Flash counter active when struck again: refresh counter (do not stack).

## Architecture

Single-bank 4KB cartridge layout, NO bankswitching. Standard 2-line kernel with NTSC timing (262 scanlines/frame, 60Hz). Source is organized by concern under src/: main.s (entry/reset/NMI vectors), kernel.s (vertical sync/blank, visible kernel, overscan), title.s (title-screen state and PF0/PF1/PF2 letter data for "TEST GAME"), input.s (joystick + INPT4/INPT5 fire button reading, edge-detect helpers), movement.s (per-player position, velocity, acceleration, friction, screen-bound clamping), missile.s (per-player missile state: active flag, x/y, dx/dy, edge despawn), collision.s (CXM0P/CXM1P latch handling, hit-flash state machine), and gamestate.s (TITLE -> PLAY transition).

Memory map (zero-page RAM): GameState (0=TITLE,1=PLAY), P0X, P0Y, P0VX, P0VY, P1X, P1Y, P1VX, P1VY (signed fixed-point: high byte position, low byte fraction), P0FireEdge, P1FireEdge, M0Active, M0X, M0Y, M0DX, M0DY, M1Active, M1X, M1Y, M1DX, M1DY, P0FlashCount, P1FlashCount, FrameCounter. ROM constants: PFCOLOR_TITLE, P0COLOR, P1COLOR, FLASH_COLOR, MAX_SPEED, ACCEL, FRICTION, MISSILE_SPEED, FLASH_FRAMES, PLAYFIELD_LEFT/RIGHT/TOP/BOTTOM.

Frame loop: VSYNC (3 LN) -> VBLANK (37 LN) [read SWCHA/INPT4/INPT5, update game state per GameState, compute fine-position registers HMP0/HMP1/HMM0/HMM1, set GRP0/GRP1 patterns, set COLUP0/COLUP1 with flash override] -> visible kernel (192 LN) [TITLE: PF-only TEST GAME banner; PLAY: 2-LN kernel rendering P0/P1 sprites + M0/M1 missiles] -> overscan (30 LN) [edge despawn missiles, decrement flash counters, update edge-detect state]. Movement uses 8-bit position + 8-bit fractional accumulator; acceleration/friction applied in VBLANK; clamp to playfield bounds before WSYNC. Missiles use sign-magnitude DX/DY (-MISSILE_SPEED..+MISSILE_SPEED) and despawn at any bound. Collisions read CXM0P (M0 vs P1) and CXM1P (M1 vs P0); on hit, set the hit player's flash counter to FLASH_FRAMES and clear the missile.

## TechDecisions

D-1 4KB no bankswitching. D-2 Hit -> brief flash, play continues. D-3 1 missile/player. D-4 Acceleration + friction + max-speed cap (fixed-point Q8.8 per axis). D-5 8-way fire direction. D-6 Edge despawn for missiles. D-7 NTSC-first (60Hz). D-8 Edge-triggered firing. D-9 Color-only player differentiation. D-10 DASM via `task build` -> `build/ataritest.bin`. D-11 Stella manual smoke test on macOS. D-12 Out-of-scope: sound, score/HUD, levels, AI, game-over, save state.

## PreImplementationGates

Toolchain: DASM installed and on PATH (`dasm -v`). Stella installed on macOS for verification. `task` (go-task) available. Project Taskfile.yml created with `build` target. Git repository initialized. PRD.md approved (DONE). specification.vbrief.json status flipped to `approved` before SPECIFICATION.md is rendered.

## P1: Foundation: toolchain + repo skeleton  `[proposed]`

Establish DASM toolchain on macOS, project directory layout, Taskfile, and a minimal kernel that boots in Stella with a black screen.

**Traces**: FR-17, FR-18, FR-19, NFR-1, NFR-2, NFR-5

## P2: Title screen  `[proposed]`

Render TEST GAME via playfield registers and add a TITLE -> PLAY state transition gated on either fire button.

**Traces**: FR-1, FR-2, FR-3, NFR-1

## P3: Player sprites and movement  `[proposed]`

Render two color-distinct sprites and implement acceleration/friction/clamping movement model driven by SWCHA.

**Traces**: FR-4, FR-5, FR-6, FR-7, FR-8, NFR-1

## P4: Missiles (8-way fire, edge-trigger, edge despawn)  `[proposed]`

Implement per-player single-active missile, fired only on fire-button press edges with non-neutral joystick, traveling in 8-way direction, despawning at screen edge.

**Traces**: FR-9, FR-10, FR-11, FR-12, FR-13, FR-14

## P5: Player-vs-player hit flash  `[proposed]`

Detect M0-vs-P1 and M1-vs-P0 collisions; trigger brief color flash on the hit player; consume the missile; do NOT modify score/lives/state.

**Traces**: FR-15, FR-16

## P6: Polish, build verification, smoke test  `[proposed]`

Verify ROM size, byte-reproducibility, NTSC stability, and document smoke-test checklist.

**Traces**: NFR-1, NFR-2, NFR-3, NFR-4, NFR-6, SM-1..SM-7
